mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly positive (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and deteriorating corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of periods where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses predictive power during recessionary troughs.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of rising retail sales and declining interest rates amplifies consumer spending power and corporate profit margins, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS growth and FEDFUNDS changes yields a Rank IC below 0.01, or positive returns fail to materialize when both conditions align.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates strengthening supply-side capacity and rising corporate output, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns during supply-constrained regimes, or the signal out-of-sample accuracy drops below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP reflects sustained economic expansion and rising corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no statistical correlation with 60-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains negative across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid monthly increases in the consumer price index trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally despite CPIAUCSL spikes above 0.4% monthly, or the signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and housing starts signals robust labor demand and durable consumer confidence, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and HOUST growth signal yields a negative Rank IC, or forward returns turn negative when both series expand concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in housing starts indicate weakening consumer balance sheets and reduced construction sector activity, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 5-day returns in over 65% of periods following two consecutive HOUST declines, or the signal loses predictive significance during rate-cut cycles.
